sentences.text: Pippi bor alene i et gammelt hus.
sentences.translation: Pippi lives alone in an old house.

words.grammar: bo (to live/reside) · bor (present) · boede (past) · a core everyday verb, distinct from leve (to be alive)
gammel · et gammelt hus (neuter) · en gammel mand (common) · irregular: gammel – gammelt – gamle
hedde (to be called) · hedder (present) · hed (past) · Hvad hedder du? = What is your name?
et hus · huset (the house) · neuter noun · husene (the houses, pl. definite)

sentences.text: Pippi har en abe og en hest.
sentences.translation: Pippi has a monkey and a horse.

words.grammar: en abe · aben (the monkey) · aber (monkeys) · aberne (the monkeys)
en hest · hesten (the horse) · heste (horses) · common gender noun

sentences.translation: The horse lives on the veranda.

words.grammar: en veranda · verandaen (the veranda) · borrowed word, always common gender

sentences.translation: The neighbours are called Tommy and Annika.

words.grammar: en nabo · naboen (the neighbour) · naboer (neighbours) · naboerne (the neighbours)

sentences.text: De ser Pippi og bliver meget overraskede.
sentences.translation: They see Pippi and become very surprised.

words.grammar: overrasket (sg.) · overraskede (pl./definite) · blive overrasket = to be/become surprised · en overraskelse = a surprise
blive (to become/stay) · bliver (present) · blev (past) · very versatile: blive glad = to become happy · blive hjemme = to stay home

sentences.text: Pippi siger: "Jeg bor her nu. Vil I være mine venner?"
sentences.translation: Pippi says: 'I live here now. Will you be my friends?'

words.grammar: en ven (m.) · venner (friends, pl.) · en veninde = female friend · gode venner = good friends
ville (will/want) · I (you plural) · Vil I? = Do you (all) want to? · I is the pl. subject pronoun (informal): rarely used in modern speech, but correct
sige (to say) · siger (present) · sagde (past) · sagt (past participle) · Hvad siger du? = What do you say?

sentences.text: Pippi laver mad til dem begge.
sentences.translation: Pippi makes food for both of them.

words.grammar: lave (to make/do) · laver mad = to cook/make food · mad (c.) = food · laver du mad? = do you cook?
begge = both · begge to = both of them · used with plural nouns · ikke begge = not both

sentences.text: Hun er stærk og sjov – den stærkeste pige i verden!
sentences.translation: She is strong and funny – the strongest girl in the world!

words.grammar: stærk · stærkere (comparative) · stærkest/den stærkeste (superlative) · en stærk vilje = a strong will
sjov (common) · sjovt (neuter) · sjove (pl./definite) · det er sjovt = it's fun · have det sjovt = to have fun
en verden · verden (the world – already definite) · i hele verden = in the whole world
